source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, the straight line $CD$ is the perpendicular bisector of the line segment $AB$, and $P$ is a point on the line $CD$. Given that the length of segment $PA$ is $3\,\text{cm}$, what is the length of segment $PB$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution}: Since line CD is the perpendicular bisector of segment AB, \\
it follows that PA=PB, \\
Since PA=3cm, \\
it follows that PB=\boxed{3cm}.

the reasoning is short and likely a single theorem application rather than a long chain of calculations
there are no answer choices and no extended numeric computation
verifiability: answer_text gives a specific numeric result: PB=3 cm
the target quantity is unambiguous and can be checked against the standard perpendicular-bisector property
answer_present=true and registry_passed=true support basic asset completeness
the expected answer is a fixed value rather than an open-ended explanation
